journal_entry.description: Meal for factory production staff - manufacturing overhead
journal_entry.rationale: The meal provided to factory production staff is a manufacturing overhead cost. Under IFRS, manufacturing overhead is accumulated in an inventory account (Work in Process or Manufacturing Overhead) as it is part of the cost of producing inventory, not a period expense. The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly indicates an asset increase (inventory/WIP), and the credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly indicates a liability increase (credit card payable). The $125 is debited to Manufacturing Overhead (which will be allocated to inventory) and credited to Credit Card Payable.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable). The meal expense itself is debited to manufacturing overhead, not credited.

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable). The meal expense itself is debited to manufacturing overhead, not credited.

output.reason: Manufacturing overhead meal = asset increase. Under IFRS, manufacturing overhead costs are capitalized as part of inventory (work-in-process or finished goods). The $125 meal cost for factory production staff is a manufacturing overhead cost that gets added to inventory value, not expensed immediately. This is an asset increase (inventory), not an expense increase.
